description: Fix api() calling refreshCsrf() before every non-GET request, which makes an extra /user/info API call that can fail independently (e.g., if rate-limited), causing the write operation to fail even though the cached CSRF token may still be valid.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/tumblr && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use cached CSRF token for writes; only refresh on 403 CSRF failure
userStories.description: In plugins/tumblr/src/tumblr-api.ts, api() calls `await refreshCsrf(auth.apiToken)` before every non-GET request, requiring a successful GET /api/v2/user/info just to get a CSRF token. If the user/info endpoint is rate-limited, the write fails even though the cached CSRF token from a previous response header is still valid. Change the logic to use the cached CSRF token from auth and only call refreshCsrf() when no cached token is available. The CSRF token is already updated from response headers after each request (lines 143-147), so the cache stays fresh without proactive refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: Non-GET requests use auth.csrfToken if it is non-empty, without calling refreshCsrf()
refreshCsrf() is only called when auth.csrfToken is empty/falsy
The response-header CSRF update (lines 143-147) still runs after every request to keep the cache fresh
The X-Csrf header is still set on all non-GET requests
